title: Sarcinula acanthodes
intro: There is not enough information in any database about the Sarcinula acanthodes.
taxonomy: Sarcinula acanthodes is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It belongs to the genus Sarcinula, which is a small group of irises native to South Africa. The species Sarcinula acanthodes is further classified as a type of geophyte, characterized by its underground storage organs and ability to regenerate new growth after flowering. This classification places Sarcinula acanthodes within the broader family of asphodels and irises. As a member of the Iridaceae family, Sarcinula acanthodes shares characteristics with other irises and asphodels.
